IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: PRESERVE THE HISPANIC CULTURE FOR THE YOUTH THROUGH EDUCATION IN THE ARTS. SEE SCHEDULE O FOR SIGNIFICANT ACTIVITIES.
IRS990.MissionDesc: PRESERVE THE HISPANIC CULTURE FOR THE YOUTH THROUGH EDUCATION IN THE ARTS. SEE SCHEDULE O FOR SIGNIFICANT ACTIVITIES.
IRS990.Desc: PRESERVATION OF HISPANIC CULTURAL TRADITIONS THROUGH NATIVE DANCE, MUSIC, ART AND SPEECH. PROGRAMS AND EVENTS TO ACHIEVE THIS INCLUDE THE MARIACHI WORKSHOP AND MARIACHI PROGRAMS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: PRESERVATION OF HISPANIC CULTURAL TRADITIONS THROUGH NATIVE DANCE, MUSIC, ART AND SPEECH. PROGRAMS AND EVENTS TO ACHIEVE THIS INCLUDE THE BALLET FOLKLORICO AND FLAMENDO PROGRAMS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: PRESERVATION OF HISPANIC CULTURAL TRADITIONS THROUGH NATIVE DANCE, MUSIC, ART AND SPEECH. SPECIAL EVENTS OR OTHER PROGRAMS TO ACHIEVE THIS INCLUDE TUTORING PROGRAMS, GOLF CLASSIC, RECITALS, CINCO DE MAYO EVENTS, A DIA DE LOS MUENTOS EVENT, AND ALL OTHER PROGRAMS.

IRS990ScheduleG.GamingManagerServicesProvTxt: OVERSEE BINGO OCCASSIONS AND REPORTING, MAINTAIN SUPPLIES, OVERSEE WORKERS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION ADDED A PRESIDENT'S CUP GOLF CLASSIC TO
HELP WITH SCHOLARSHIPS AND GENERAL OPERATING EXPENSES. WE
THINK IT MAY TAKE THREE YEARS TO GET THIS EVENT WHERE IT
SHOULD BE AS IT IS HELD AT THE SAME TIME AS THE PARADE.
IT HELPS KICK OFF THE HISPANIC HERITAGE MONTH FESTIVITIES.
DIA DE LOS MUERTOS WAS ADDED IN A JOINT EVENT WITH MIDLAND
COUNTY LIBRARIES. WE SEARCHED FOR SPONSORS TO HELP BUY
THE TREES "ARBOLITOS DE VIDA" (TREE OF LIFE). ELEMENTARY
SCHOOLS WOULD SCHEDULE A LIBRARY TOUR AND WERE INVITED TO
DECORATE THE EIGHT TREES WHICH TURNED OUT BEAUTIFULLY. WE
INVITED THE CLASSES TO ALSO BUILD ALTARS TO DEDICATE TO
LOVED ONES, CAUSES, AND EVENTS.
ONCE THE FORM 990 IS PREPARED, IT IS REVIEWED BY THE
PRESIDENT AND TREASURER OF THE BOARD. THEN IT IS
SUBMITTED TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR REVIEW AND
APPROVAL PRIOR TO THE FILING DUE DATE OF THE RETURN.
THE INFORMATION BELOW REFERS TO FORM 990 PART I L1:
THE BALLET FOLKLORICO "NUESTRA HERENCIA" IS THE PROGRAM
THAT STARTED HCCM. SINCE ITS INCEPTION THE FOLKLORICO
PROGRAM IS WHAT HAS KEPT THE ORGANIZATION ALIVE. THE
PROGRAM IS FOR STUDENTS AGES FIVE TO EIGHTEEN. STUDENTS
THAT GRADUATE ARE INVITED TO BECOME MENTORS FOR THE
YOUNGER STUDENTS. DIFFERENT REGIONS ARE TAUGHT TO THE
BEGINNER, INTERMEDIATE, AND ADVANCED GROUPS. HCCM
PROVIDES ALL THE INSTRUCTORS AND COSTUMES. IF A FAMILY
CANNOT AFFORD THE SHOES, HCCM WILL PROVIDE THEM. THE
OBJECT OF THE DANCE PROGRAM IS TO BUILD ONE'S CONFIDENCE
AND SELF-ESTEEM. MANY OF THE STUDENTS ARE VERY SHY AND
TEND TO KEEP WITHIN THIER OWN SPACE. HCCM BRINGS OUT THE
BEAUTY IN EVERY CHILD. THEY ARE SO PROUD OF THEMSELVES
WHEN THEY SEE THAT THEY CAN MASTER THE FOOTWORK AND
CHOREOGRAPHY. THE BALLET PROGRAM CONTINUES TO GROW AND
FLOURISH WITH TWO INSTRUCTORS WHO PRIDE THEMSELVES IN
MAKING EVERY SURE STUDENT HAS A WONDERFUL EXPERIENCE. THE
THE BALLET HAS PERFORMED ALL OVER MIDLAND AT MANY EVENTS:
CELEBRATE HISPANIC EVENTS SUCH AS CINCO DE MAYO, DIEZ Y
SIES, QUINCERENAS, JAMAICA'S, CELEBRATION OF THE ARTS,
CAF, JUNIOR LEAGUE, MIDLAND CHAMBER, MEX-TEX, FIESTA WEST
TEXAS IN ODESSA, SEVERAL EVENTS AT MIDLAND COLLEGE,
MIDLAND COUNTY LIBRARIES, AND PROGRAMS BY MIDLAND ISD.
BALLET FLAMENCO -- HCCM BEGAN THE FLAMENCO CLASSES IN
NOVEMBER 2014. THE ORGANIZATION WAS FORTUNATE TO FIND A
FLAMENCO INSTRUCTOR THAT HAD RELOCATED TO MIDLAND FROM
SAN ANTONIO. THERE ARE TWO CLASSES: BEGINNERS AND
INTERMEDIATE. THEY ARE TAUGHT ON TUESDAYS AND THURSDAYS.
MORE INDIVIDUALS ARE RECOGNIZING THAT THESE CLASSES ARE
AVAILABLE WHICH HAS RESULTED IN NEW GROWTH WITHIN THIS
DIFFERENT HISPANIC CULTURAL PROGRAM.
CINCO DE MAYO CELEBRATIONS - HCCM INVITES ALL THE SCHOOLS
TO COME CELEBRATE THE CINCO DE MAYO AT HCCM'S CENTER.
THIS YEAR OVER 2000 STUDENTS ATTENDED. HCCM ORGANIZED A
DISPLAY FOR THE STUDENTS ON CINCO DE MAYO AND WHAT TOOK
PLACE LEADING TO THAT DAY. THEY WERE SERVED BEAN BURRITOS
WITH FRUIT, CHIPS AND WATER WHILE OBSERVING ENTERTAINMENT
IN THE PARK. HCCM PAYS FOR ALL OF THIS. HCCM BALLETS
AND MARIACHI PERFORMED. WE ALSO BROUGHT IN A "BANDA DE
GUERRA Y ESCOLTA" FROM JUAREZ,MEXICO TO ENTERTAIN THE
STUDENTS WHO WERE SO EXCITED TO SEE THEM. WE HAD LOCAL
TALENT THIS YEAR AND PROVIDED MORE FOOD BOOTHS FOR THE
STUDENTS. IT WAS A BIG HIT WITH EVERYONE ENJOYING A
TRADITION THAT HAS BEEN PRESERVED FOR CENTURIES.
HCCM ANNUAL RECITAL -- HCCM HAD IT'S RECITAL AT THE
MIDLAND COMMUNITY THEATRE FOR ITS 21ST ANNIVERSARY EVENT.
HCCA MIDLAND WELCOMED THE MARIACHI GROUP TO THE EVENT.
OVER 450 INDIVIDUALS ATTENDED THE RECITAL.
HCCM MIDLAND MARIACHI WORKSHOP AND CONCERT -- THIS IS OUR
BIGGEST EVENT. THE MARIACHI WORKSHOP BEGAN IN 2003.
DIFFERENT VENUES HAVE BEEN ADDED THROUGHOUT THE YEARS.
HCCM BRINGS IN A PROFESSIONAL MARIACHI TO CONDUCT A THREE
DAY WORKSHOP FOR STUDENTS FROM THE FIFTH TO THE TWELTH
GRADES. THIS YEAR WE BROUGHT IN THE UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS
RIO GRANDE VALLEY "MARIACHI ATZLAN" TO CONDUCT THE
INSTRUCTION. "MARIACHI ATZLAN" IS THE BEST. THEIR SKILLS
AND TALENTS ARE ABOVE OTHER MARIACHIS. ALL THE MUSICIANS
ARE STUDENTS WHO HAVE A PASSION FOR MUSIC. THE MARIACHI
FOCUS ON THE MUSIC BY TEACHING TRADITIONAL STYLES AND
METHODS OF A PROFESSIONAL MARIACHI GROUP WITH THE GOAL TO
TEACH STUDENTS AT LEAST EIGHT SONGS IN A THREE DAY PERIOD.
WE FOCUS ON EDUCATION AND WANT TO SHOW MARIACHI MUSIC
HELPS THESE INDIVIDUALS FUND THEIR EDUCATION.
THE NOCHE DE MARIACHI RECEPTION WAS A GREAT SUCCESS.
SPONSORS, CITY OFFICIALS, AND HCCM VOLUNTEERS ARE
INVITED. HCCM INCORPORATED A SILENT AUCTION TO HELP WITH
MOST OF THE STUDENTS PAY THE $75 FEE, BUT SEVERAL FROM
MIDLAND AND ODESSA ARE GIVEN SCHOLARSHIPS TO ATTEND.
MARIACHI AZTLAN PERFORMED AT THE RECEPTION. EVERYONE
ENJOYED THE PRIVATE CONCERT AND THE FRIENDSHIPS THAT HAVE
DEVELOPED THROUGHOUT THE YEARS.
AT THE END IS THE GRAND CONCERT. THIS CONCERT HIGHTLIGHTS
THE STUDENTS AS THEY PERFORM ALL THE MUSIC THEY LEARNED.
THE MAIN PERFORMANCE FOR THIS EVENT IS THE "MARIACHI
ESTRELLA." THE MARIACHI ATZLAN SELECTS THE BEST AND
THOSE WHO HAVE EXCELED FROM THE WORKSHOP. THESE STUDENTS
PERFORM AS A GROUP. THIS IS THE ONLY TIME SOME OF THEM
HAVE PERFORMED BEFORE A LARGE AUDIENCE. SEEING THE FACES
OF BOTH STUDENTS AND PARENTS IS AN AWESOME FEELING. ALL
THE STUDENTS FINISH WITH WONDERFUL PRIDE IN THEMSELVES
AND THIER CULTURE.
DIEZ Y SEIS PARADE -- HCCM MIDLAND HAS HELD THIS PARADE
FOR LAST SEVETEEN YEARS. IT HAS ONLY BEEN CANCELLED ONCE
WHICH WAS ON SEPTEMBER 11, 2001 AND IN SEPTEMBER 2014
WHEN IT WAS CANCELLED DUE TO BAD WEATHER. THIS YEAR'S
EVENT WENT OFF BEATIFULLY.
CHRISTMAS AT HCCM -- THE BOARD HAS ALWAYS HAD A CHRISTMAS
PARTY FOR THE STUDENTS AND THEIR FAMILIES. WITH OUR NEW
BUILDING, HCCM IS NOW ABLE TO HAVE A FORMAL EVENT. OUR
AND VOLUNTEERS SPEND A COUPLE OF WEEKS DECORATING THE
AUDITORIUM. THIS YEAR'S THEME WAS A "A CANDY CANE
CHRISTMAS." MR AND MRS SANTA CLAUS WERE BROUGHT IN TO
ENJOY THE MUSIC AND DIFFERENT KINDS OF SPANISH FOODS.
THIS IS ONE WAY FOR THE BOARD TO THANK THE STUDENTS AND
THEIR FAMILIES FOR ALL THEY DO FOR THE ORGANIZATION.
MARIACHI PROGRAM -- WHAT STARTED AS A WORKSHOP FOR THE
SUMMER OF 2016 BECAME A REALITY AGAIN FOR HCCM. THE
INTEREST TO CONTINUE THE MARIACHI PROGRAM CAME FROM TWO
PASSED MEMBERS OF OUR MARIACHI GROUP "NUEVO GERNERACION."
WE BEGAN WITH A WORKSHOP AND NOW WE ARE INTO THE SECOND
AS A PROGRAM. WE CURRENTLY HAVE FIFTEEN MEMBERS WITH THE
HOPES TO INCREASE IT BY AT LEAST TEN MEMBERS MORE THIS
YEAR.
